mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms robust consumer demand and capital expenditure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 20-day returns in > 55% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production indices reflect strong manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance against zero.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 3 percent annually, monetary authorities tighten policy, raising discount rates and forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns during tightening periods with p-value > 0.1 in t-test.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth exceeding 200 thousand signals strong labor market expansion and rising corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered months over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real gross domestic product and retail sales validates broad-based economic demand, lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Combined positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in > 55% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment indicates supply-side contraction and recession risk, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0 and UNRATE month-over-month change > 0.1
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in > 60% of triggered cases over a rolling 3-year evaluation window.
